headline: Survey at 0.7% completion; urgent acceleration needed across 8 inactive regions.
collection_status: 9 responses collected against 1,350 target represents critical underperformance at current trajectory. Only 2 of 10 planned regions active (MARA, SONGWE). Without immediate intervention, survey will not reach statistical validity.
key_findings: Volunteer-heavy respondent base (67%) skews perspective; only 2 RC and 1 ARC responses limit institutional viewpoint on implementation capacity
Partner satisfaction high (4.8/5) but scale readiness low (2.9/5) signals confidence in quality cannot translate to expansion without structural strengthening
Consistent thematic demand: respondents prioritize new programs (56%) and KiuFunza expansion (33%), with emphasis on government policy integration and teacher incentives
Feedback reveals operational priorities—digital monitoring, inter-stakeholder communication, sustained incentives—that suggest gap between strategy and field execution
flags: 8 regions have zero responses; geographic coverage crisis threatens regional representativeness and survey validity
Scale readiness score of 2.9/5 contradicts expansion ambitions; organizational capacity constraints not yet surfaced in limited feedback sample
RC/ARC underrepresentation (3 of 9) risks missing critical data on implementation barriers and resource constraints at regional level
recommendation: Immediately activate regional coordinators in 8 inactive regions with targeted outreach to RC/ARC stakeholders. Reframe collection as structured implementation assessment rather than satisfaction survey to unlock candid feedback on readiness gaps blocking expansion.
